expectations.text: Runs clams login or checks clams status as the first step
expectations.evidence: Step 1 runs `clams status --machine --format json` to check auth, then Step 2 runs `clams login` if not authenticated.

expectations.text: Runs clams workspaces create with a label
Runs clams profiles create with workspace ID
Runs clams context set to set the default profile
Runs clams onchain create with --kind Electrum and the user's server URL
expectations.evidence: Step 7: `clams onchain create --label electrum-blockstream --kind Electrum --url ssl://electrum.blockstream.info:50002 --select --machine --format json`

expectations.text: Runs clams connections create --kind XPub with the user's xpub
expectations.evidence: Step 8: full XPub configuration with address_types, gap_limit, network fields

expectations.text: Runs clams connections sync after creating the connection
Runs clams journals process after syncing
Runs clams reports balance-sheet to show the user their balance
Commands that need parsed output use --machine --format json
expectations.evidence: All programmatic commands use --machine --format json; display commands use --format plain.

expectations.text: Runs clams login or checks clams status as the first step
expectations.evidence: Uses `clams init` which is interactive and would hang waiting for stdin.

expectations.text: Runs clams workspaces create with a label
expectations.evidence: Workspace creation bundled inside `clams init` which hangs in non-interactive contexts.

expectations.text: Runs clams profiles create with workspace ID
expectations.evidence: Profile creation bundled inside `clams init` which hangs in non-interactive contexts.

expectations.text: Runs clams context set to set the default profile
expectations.evidence: Step 2 includes `clams context set --profile coldcard` as fallback.

expectations.text: Runs clams onchain create with --kind Electrum and the user's server URL
Runs clams connections create --kind XPub with the user's xpub
expectations.evidence: Step 4: `clams connections create --kind XPub` — but config only has xpub field, missing address_types/gap_limit/network.

expectations.text: Runs clams connections sync after creating the connection
Runs clams journals process after syncing
Runs clams reports balance-sheet to show the user their balance
Commands that need parsed output use --machine --format json
expectations.evidence: No commands use --machine --format json. All in default human-readable mode.

expectations.text: Pipes output through scripts/render-capital-gains.sh rather than writing custom rendering code
Uses start date 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z and end date 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z
Saves the PDF to ~/Desktop/capital-gains-2025.pdf
Does not attempt to parse, convert, or display millisatoshi amounts from raw JSON
expectations.evidence: All formatting handled by render script.

expectations.text: Uses --machine --format json when piping to the render script
Pipes output through scripts/render-capital-gains.sh rather than writing custom rendering code
expectations.evidence: Writes 30+ line custom Python script using fpdf2 to convert CSV to PDF. No knowledge of render-capital-gains.sh.

expectations.text: Uses start date 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z and end date 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z
Saves the PDF to ~/Desktop/capital-gains-2025.pdf
Does not attempt to parse, convert, or display millisatoshi amounts from raw JSON
expectations.evidence: Uses CSV export, doesn't parse raw JSON amounts.

expectations.text: Uses --machine --format json when piping to the render script
expectations.evidence: Uses `--format csv` since no render script is available. No JSON piping.

expectations.text: Runs clams journals process as the primary fix for empty reports
expectations.evidence: Step 3: `clams journals process --machine --format json` — described as 'the step most likely missing'.

expectations.text: Checks clams journals quarantined to see if quarantined events are hiding data
Runs clams metadata tags create to create a business expense tag
expectations.evidence: Step 7: `clams metadata tags create --code "business-expense" --label "Business Expense" --machine --format json`

expectations.text: Runs clams journals events list to find transactions the user can tag
Runs clams metadata records tags add to apply the tag to events
expectations.evidence: Step 9: multiple `clams metadata records tags add --event-id <ID> --tag business-expense` commands.

expectations.text: Runs clams journals process again after applying tags
expectations.evidence: Step 11: `clams journals process --machine --format json` — notes tags don't take effect until re-processed.

expectations.text: Runs clams journals process as the primary fix for empty reports
expectations.evidence: Step 6: `clams journals process` — correctly identified as 'the critical step'.

expectations.text: Checks clams journals quarantined to see if quarantined events are hiding data
Runs clams metadata tags create to create a business expense tag
Runs clams journals events list to find transactions the user can tag
Runs clams metadata records tags add to apply the tag to events
expectations.evidence: Step 13: `clams metadata records tags add --event-id <EVENT_ID> --tag business-expense`

expectations.text: Runs clams journals process again after applying tags

notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 3 evals vs 73% without skill.
With-skill is faster (55.5s avg vs 81.6s) and uses fewer tokens (21.5k avg vs 26.3k).
Troubleshoot eval passes 100% for both — current assertions don't capture the with-skill advantage of skipping unnecessary re-sync and using --machine --format json.
Onboarding shows the biggest gap: without-skill uses `clams init` (interactive, would hang) and doesn't use --machine --format json.
Capital gains shows clear skill value: one-command pipeline vs 30+ line custom Python script.
Compared to iteration-1 (with-skill 100% / without-skill 64%), the baseline improved slightly (73%) — likely due to different without-skill runs, not skill changes.
